notes: Twelve walks, June to September. weather drives the tint and the radio burst. Neighbor scenes are walk-ins · stiff, earnest, four people deep · visibly the rehearsal for Pirate Summer's web. Stakes appear week 6. The hearing is after walk 10.

walks.radio: K-K-H. OH-VER-CAST. EBB AT TWO. GOOD MORN-ING, FLATS.

scenes.jules: a kid in their early twenties sits on the boardwalk rail, watching the flat like it's television. 'you're new. i'm jules. i'm not doing anything. that's on purpose.'

walks.radio: K-K-H. FOG TO NOON. FLOOD AT FIVE. MIND THE CHAN-NEL.

scenes.ruth: ruth, hauling crab pots over the rail of a boat that sits low. 'watch your boots. the flat keeps what it takes.' she says it like a greeting because it is one.

walks.radio: K-K-H. SUN. YES. WE CHECKED TWICE. SUN.

scenes.cole: an old man on the bluff path, walking like an inspection. 'cole. retired county. that channel silts a quarter-inch a year and i could tell you why but nobody's asked me anything in nine years.'

walks.radio: K-K-H. OH-VER-CAST. NEAP TIDE. QUI-ET WEEK.

scenes.ames: mrs. ames sweeps a boardwalk that doesn't need it. 'i own from the rail to the third plank past the radio. people forget.' she isn't sweeping. she's counting.

walks.radio: K-K-H. FOG. THE FRY ARE LEAV-ING. WE WILL MISS THEM.

scenes.jules: jules, at the channel edge. 'the fry go out this month. everyone acts like it's nothing. it's the whole calendar if you're a fry.'

walks.radio: K-K-H. OH-VER-CAST. THERE ARE... STAKES. ON THE FLAT. THAT IS ALL WE KNOW.

scenes.ruth: ruth stands by the first orange stake, arms crossed. 'marina traffic needs eight feet. my boat needs six and doesn't get it half the year. i know what side i'm on and i don't love it.'
scenes.cole: cole reads the stake's tag through his glasses, twice. 'county survey. i know the form number. i wrote the form number.' he leaves before you can ask.

walks.radio: K-K-H. SUN A-GAIN. TWICE ONE SUM-MER. WRITE IT DOWN.

scenes.ames: mrs. ames, by the stakes, performing neutrality. 'i haven't decided. commerce is good for the boardwalk. so is the view. i am counting.' she looks at your journal when she says it.
scenes.jules: jules: 'ruth taught me knots. cole taught me why the channel bends. nobody teaches you which side to be on. you just watch until you know.'

walks.radio: K-K-H. STORM. FIRST OF THE YEAR. THE FLAT DRINKS FIRST.

scenes.cole: cole, in the rain, not going in. 'you want to know why i'm against it. i signed the '81 dredge upcoast. the flats there... there aren't flats there. i signed it.' the rain takes the rest.

walks.radio: K-K-H. OH-VER-CAST. PE-TI-TION WEATHER, WE HEAR.

scenes.ruth: ruth: 'heard there's a petition walking around. don't bring it to my boat.' a pause. 'bring it anyway. i won't sign it. i want to see it.'

walks.radio: K-K-H. FOG. THE HEAR-ING IS THURS-DAY. FOLD-ING CHAIRS.

scenes.jules: jules, quiet for once. 'they give the public three minutes each. three minutes. you've been out here all summer. that's got to count as standing, right? looking at a thing?'

walks.radio: K-K-H. OH-VER-CAST. THE COUN-TY HAS SPO-KEN. THE FLAT HAS NOT.

scenes.ames: mrs. ames sweeps, actually sweeps this time. 'well. it's decided. boardwalk's still mine, rail to third plank.' whatever happened, she has finished counting.

walks.radio: K-K-H. SUN. LAST WALK OF THE SEA-SON. THANK YOU FOR LIS-TEN-ING.

scenes.ruth: ruth coils line, boat riding however the county left the water. 'see you next summer. or don't. the flat doesn't take attendance.' it is the warmest thing she has said.

hearing.intro: folding chairs. a county seal on a portable stand. three minutes each. the clerk reads the item number and the room does not breathe.
hearing.no_standing: you stand up with nothing but a summer of walking. the chair asks if you have observations to enter. you don't · not written, not illuminated, not evidence. you sit down. looking almost counted.
hearing.statement_prefix: you enter your journal pages into the record, one by one, and the room gets quieter with each ·

dredged.title: DREDGED
dredged.text: the county votes for the marina. the dredge works november. the winter card shows a straight channel and your journal pages fading at the edges, as if the ink knew.

blocked.title: BLOCKED
blocked.text: the county tables the dredge indefinitely. the flat stays as you knew it. ruth no longer waves. some winters cost a neighbor.

compromise.title: THE COMPROMISE
compromise.text: half-dredge · the channel deepened to the bend, the flat left past it. the winter card refuses judgment. downstream, years from now, a tide gate will manage exactly this half-channel, and a studio will make a game about it.
